given: Two verified immutable release directories and an isolated installation root
when: The release lifecycle advances the current link and retains its predecessor
acceptance: Installation switches an immutable current release

A double-click Windows installation asks the user to choose one absolute Personal Agent root and keeps immutable Core, mutable Workspace data, installation staging, upgrades, rollback and uninstall state under that selected root; explicit CLI installation remains non-interactive and macOS behavior is unchanged

Fresh Windows and macOS installation opens the local Setup Center in the Tauri desktop shell; fresh Linux installation uses a version-bound one-line GitHub Release installer, ships no desktop shell or WebView dependency, enables a persistent systemd user service, and exposes Setup Center only through loopback for SSH forwarding

The desktop silently starts managed Cloud enrollment, Sites domain allocation and Agent mail identity synchronization; only a failed or cancelled silent attempt exposes manual recovery and explicit disconnect suppresses automatic reconnection

Sites and local mail expose a keyboard-accessible custom-domain choice beside the platform-domain action; the three-step self-hosted flow clearly requires a separate public Linux server, shows a copyable version-bound GitHub Release installer whose self-extracting asset embeds the Relay and is covered by public checksums and signing evidence, accepts the server-issued Relay key in a password field, binds only the key digest and domain in its approved R2 fingerprint, keeps plaintext only in local Secrets while the server persists only a digest, configures outbound WSS without WireGuard or local administrator rights, maps the apex and Space subdomains across the installation, and commits only after final HTTPS content or real local-mail evidence; without a verified platform or custom domain, Sites and local mail remain warning-state “未生效” connections and are excluded from the effective view even though their local processes and data are preserved

The Windows and macOS desktop shell uses the system WebView and owns the client lifecycle; Linux has no desktop runtime and remains continuously supervised by systemd, with every platform bound to current/previous rollback
The local Console and main Agent share one digest-bound update plan, verified platform artifact, platform-appropriate native handoff, and persisted restart result

A pre-release self-contained candidate can create a ten-minute digest-bound plan only when its commit, manifest, checksums, SBOM, candidate security metadata and platform agree; it reuses the platform-appropriate native handoff and immutable installer, records candidateAssetRuntime=true, and never satisfies public Release acceptance

Applying executable updates normally supports authenticated local approval; a registered Owner-initiated product-development flow authorizes the exact digest-bound plan by policy without a second local confirmation

Managed Cloud and self-hosted reverse tunnels delegate the full Web and mobile route set to the local gateway; direct loopback access needs no login, tunneled routes on the explicit public whitelist remain public, every other tunneled path requires the host-scoped access session before normal route resolution, and password reset invalidates existing remote sessions
Browser and CLI recovery remain available
Platform releases bundle the pinned OpenCLI production graph without lifecycle scripts or customer-side npm installation, while Chrome retains explicit Browser Bridge permission
